title: A retargeted recombinase for precise insertion of large DNA - Nature Biotechnology
gist: A News & Views piece describes an approach to retarget the large serine recombinase Bxb1 so it can precisely integrate large DNA payloads at chosen genomic loci, a programmable alternative to CRISPR-based knock-in methods; the underlying research article is paywalled behind Nature+/journal subscription.

insights.claim: A newly reported approach retargets the large serine recombinase Bxb1 so that it can precisely integrate large DNA payloads at desired genomic loci, rather than only at its native attB/attP recognition sites.
insights.speaker: Frank Buchholz
insights.so_what: Programmable large serine recombinases could offer a scarless, single-step alternative to CRISPR-plus-homology-directed-repair for inserting large gene cassettes (e.g., CAR constructs, large transgenes), which is relevant for cell-therapy manufacturing platforms.
insights.quote: An approach to retarget the large serine recombinase Bxb1 enables precise integration of large DNA payloads at desired loci.

insights.claim: The commentary is illustrated with a figure titled 'Reprogramming Bxb1 recombinase for programmable genome integration,' indicating the underlying advance is about engineering Bxb1's target specificity rather than its catalytic mechanism.
insights.speaker: Frank Buchholz
insights.so_what: Reprogrammable target specificity (versus fixed att-site dependence) is the commercially relevant unlock for using Bxb1 as a generic genome-writing tool across arbitrary loci.
insights.quote: Fig. 1: Reprogramming Bxb1 recombinase for programmable genome integration.

insights.claim: The commentary's author, Frank Buchholz, is a co-founder with equity stakes in three companies working in adjacent recombinase/gene-editing space — Eupheria Biotech, Provirex, and Seamless Tx — and serves as scientific advisor to all three.
insights.speaker: Frank Buchholz
insights.so_what: These three named companies are direct commercial vehicles for large-serine-recombinase technology and are worth tracking for licensing deals or product announcements tied to programmable Bxb1-style integration.
insights.quote: F.B. is a co-founder of Eupheria Biotech, Provirex and Seamless Tx and holds equity in these companies. He also serves as a scientific advisor to these companies.
